content.problemStatement:
Chef finished inviting all of his friends. Now it is time to get a tree in his house and decorate it for the party. He wants to use the most beautiful tree ever.

The tree Chef chose has NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN) and it is rooted at node 111. For each valid iii, the initial colour of the iii-th node is CiC_iCi‚Äã and there is a value AiA_iAi‚Äã written in that node. Chef calculates the beauty of his tree as ‚àëi=1NAi‚ãÖFi\sum_{i=1}^N A_i \cdot F_i‚àëi=1N‚ÄãAi‚Äã‚ãÖFi‚Äã, where FiF_iFi‚Äã denotes the number of distinct colours of nodes in the subtree of the iii-th node (including this node).

Now, Chef is trying to improve the beauty of the tree ‚Äï he wants to make QQQ updates, where in each update, he chooses a node aaa and an integer xxx and changes the colour of the node aaa to xxx. After each update, find the beauty of the tree. Note that the updates are permanent, i.e. they affect all subsequent updates.
constraints: 1‚â§Ai,Ci‚â§N1 \le A_i, C_i \le N1‚â§Ai‚Äã,Ci‚Äã‚â§N for each valid iii
the graph described on the input is a tree
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
Each of the following N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting an edge between nodes uuu and vvv.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1‚Äã,C2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,CN‚Äã.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
Each of the following QQQ lines contains two space-separated integers aaa and xxx describing a query.
content.outputFormat: For each update, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the beauty of the tree after this update is performed.
